title: quercus × palaeolithicola
intro: Quercus × palaeolithicola is a hybrid plant species, resulting from the cross between Quercus robur and Quercus pedunculata, commonly known as the Paleolithic oak or hybrid white oak. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic compounds. The health benefits associated with Quercus × palaeolithicola include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Quercus × palaeolithicola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
